data_coverage_summary: Onfleet exposes dispatch and last-mile delivery APIs for organizations, administrators, workers, teams, hubs, destinations, recipients, tasks, containers, orders, route plans, optimization runs, and delivery webhooks.
buyer_guidance: Use Onfleet when you need a dispatch and proof-of-delivery API for last-mile operations, including task creation, assignment, optimization, recipient communications, and webhook-driven delivery status updates.
overview: Onfleet's API guide states that everything starts with a task, with tasks linked to destinations, recipients, administrators, and workers. The public reference also exposes CRUD and workflow operations for workers, teams, hubs, destinations, recipients, tasks, containers, orders, route plans, and route optimization, plus webhooks with more than 10 event triggers and proof-of-delivery retrieval. That makes it suitable for operational asset or order records and delivery-event telemetry, with documented writeback across the dispatch lifecycle. Asset data is Supported. Telemetry data is Supported. Writeback is Supported.

available_data.category: Dispatch master data and delivery objects
available_data.description: The public reference covers the core records needed to model delivery operations and maintain them over time.
data_points: Workers, worker schedules, and worker assigned tasks
Tasks, containers, orders, and route plans

available_data.category: Assignments, delivery progress, and event callbacks
available_data.description: Onfleet documents workflow APIs for assigning work and returning delivery progress to downstream systems.
data_points: Task completion requirements and proof-of-delivery artifacts
Batch task creation and asynchronous batch-job status
Webhook event triggers and webhook payload examples
Route optimization runs and optimization status

ingest_considerations: Onfleet recommends validating addresses before task creation and supports metadata fields for storing external identifiers and structured custom data on supported objects.
Webhook delivery should be treated as the primary event signal for downstream updates, especially for order-management, customer-facing, and delivery-tracking applications.
No exact public machine-readable API description was confirmed during this review, so the ReadMe-based reference remains the evidence anchor until Onfleet exposes a downloadable JSON or YAML spec.

source_evidence.evidence_notes: The Onfleet quickstart and reference document workers, teams, hubs, destinations, recipients, tasks, orders, route plans, optimization, proof-of-delivery retrieval, metadata, and webhooks with more than 10 event triggers.
